channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Proteas coach Mashimbyi announces coaching staff changes ahead of India T20I series
item.description: Proteas Women's head coach Mandla Mashimbyi has made significant changes to the coaching staff following a disappointing tour of New Zealand. He emphasised the need for fresh voices, energy, and persp...

item.title: Cuba Faces Tourism Decline Amid Fuel and Power Shortages Caused by U.S. Sanctions
item.description: In Cuba, tourism is experiencing a significant downturn, particularly in eco-tourism hubs such as the small town on the edge of the Zapata Swamp. This drop in visitors has compounded the economic stru...

item.title: Excerpt from Sian Hughes’ ‘No Such Thing as Monday’: A haunting tale of memory and family bonds
item.description: This excerpt from Sian Hughes’ novel No Such Thing as Monday explores the emotional complexities of a woman named Steffie as she confronts the memories surrounding her estranged sister and their troub...

item.title: Welsh racehorse trainer Evan Williams sentenced to three years for grievous bodily harm in dog-walking incident
item.description: Evan Williams, a prominent Welsh racehorse trainer known for his success in the Grand National, has been sentenced to three years in prison for assaulting a man with a hockey stick. The incident occur...

item.title: Boosting Financial Literacy: A Key to South Africa's Economic Wellbeing
item.description: A recent article highlights the crucial role of financial literacy in ensuring financial security and making informed decisions, particularly within South Africa where only 51% of adults are considere...

item.title: Kenya plans strategic fuel reserve in Mombasa to strengthen energy security
item.description: The Kenyan government is moving to improve the country’s energy security by exploring the establishment of a strategic petroleum reserve, with Mombasa identified as the proposed location for a conting...

item.title: South African Presidency Refutes Elon Musk’s Claims About Farmer Persecution
item.description: South Africa’s government has publicly criticised Elon Musk for spreading false information regarding the treatment of white farmers in the country. Musk, born in Pretoria, retweeted a video showing a...

item.title: Disabling Ant2 Protein Enhances T Cell Function to Combat Cancer
item.description: Researchers at the Hebrew University of Jerusalem, in collaboration with scientists from Philipps University of Marburg and MD Anderson Cancer Center, have discovered a method to enhance the immune sy...

item.title: Kenya launches security sector reforms to strengthen accountability and improve justice delivery
item.description: The Government of Kenya has initiated fresh efforts to overhaul its security sector in a bid to improve efficiency, accountability, and public trust in law enforcement institutions. The Head of Public...

item.title: Elk Island Bison to Enhance Alaska’s Conservation Efforts
item.description: Elk Island National Park in Alberta is contributing to Alaskan conservation efforts by transferring 44 wood bison to the state. This marks a significant step in re-establishing a sustainable populatio...

item.title: Molo Residents Raise Alarm Over Livestock Theft and Poor Security
item.description: Residents of Sion village in Molo Constituency have raised alarm over rising cases of livestock theft that have left many families in fear and economic distress. The latest incident saw thieves break ...

item.title: Tanzanian Students Recount Escape from War-Torn Iran
item.description: Two Tanzanian sisters, Fatma Hassan Nasser and UmmyKulthum Nasser, were among several Tanzanian students studying in Iran who had to flee after the outbreak of war on February 28, 2026. Fatma, a law s...

item.title: BBC investigation reveals ongoing infection control failures at Taunsa THQ Hospital despite HIV outbreak response
item.description: According to a BBC Eye Investigations report, serious malpractices in the children ward of Taunsa Tehsil Headquarters Hospital in Punjab continued even months after the facility was linked to a major ...

item.title: S&P 500 Rebounds Beyond Prewar Levels as Investors Question Market Sustainability
item.description: The Wall Street Journal article discusses how the S&P 500 has climbed back above the levels it held before geopolitical tensions escalated, marking a significant rebound for U.S. equities. Analysts at...

item.title: Analysis of Kerala’s Reported High Voter Turnout Figures and Their Interpretation
item.description: The original news article from NDTV appears to be inaccessible due to a server-side access restriction, and its full content could not be retrieved for analysis. The link indicates that the piece focu...

item.title: Ali Isa, Minority Whip, Officially Leaves PDP Over Internal Crisis
item.description: Ali Isa, wey be the Minority Whip for House of Representatives, don officially resign from the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP). Him resignation letter, wey e write on April 14, 2026, dey addressed to t...

item.title: Global Police Leaders Gather in Poland to Combat Antisemitism and Hate Crimes
item.description: In April 2026, around 130 law enforcement leaders from Europe and North America convened in Kraków, Poland, for a pioneering initiative linked to the International March of the Living. The delegation ...

item.title: Parliament Energy Committee Launches Inquiry into Fuel Quality and Import Irregularities in Kenya
item.description: The National Assembly Committee on Energy in Kenya has initiated a detailed fact-finding mission aimed at addressing growing concerns over fuel shortages and the quality of imported petroleum products...

item.title: China Criticises US Blockade of Iranian Ports Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: China has sharply criticised the United States for its recent blockade of Iranian ports, describing the move as 'dangerous and irresponsible.' The blockade, implemented despite a fragile two-week ceas...

item.title: Tankers Navigate Strait of Hormuz Despite US Blockade on Iranian Ports
item.description: Despite a newly imposed US blockade on ports in Iran, several vessels, including those sanctioned by the US, successfully passed through the crucial Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday, April 14th, 2026. This...
